description:
You are the on-call incident responder.  
    A copy of the authentication log database has been placed in  
    /home/user/ir_breach/breach.db

    1. Confirm that you can open the database with the sqlite3 CLI and verify that it contains a single table named auth_log with five columns (id, timestamp, username, src_ip, action).
    2. Using only standard shell tools that are available in a minimal Linux installation (bash utilities and sqlite3), identify every row whose action value is exactly the string FAIL.
    3. Export those rows—*and only those rows*—to the file  
       /home/user/ir_breach/findings.log

       Output format requirements (the test-suite will compare the file byte-for-byte):
       • One log entry per line.  
       • Column order and delimiter:  
         TIMESTAMP ␣|␣ USERNAME ␣|␣ SRC_IP ␣|␣ ACTION  
         (That is, a single space on each side of the vertical bar.)  
       • The lines must be sorted in ascending chronological order based on the TIMESTAMP column contained in the database.
       • Do not write a header line.
       Example of a correctly formatted line:  
         2023-08-10 05:05:43 | root | 203.0.113.45 | FAIL

    4. Still inside /home/user/ir_breach create a short plain-text executive summary called incident_report.txt with exactly three lines, each in the form key-colon-space-value, *no* extra spaces and *no* blank lines:

           Total Failed Logins: <integer>
           Unique Suspicious IPs: <integer>
           Top Offender IP: <ip-address> (<attempt-count> attempts)

       Definitions you must follow:
       • “Total Failed Logins” is the total number of FAIL rows you exported.  
       • “Unique Suspicious IPs” is the number of distinct SRC_IP values that appear among those FAIL rows.  
       • “Top Offender IP” is the SRC_IP that appears the most among FAIL rows, followed by the exact number of its FAIL attempts inside parentheses.

       Use the same numeric values that you obtained from the database; do not hard-code guesses.

    5. Make sure both /home/user/ir_breach/findings.log and /home/user/ir_breach/incident_report.txt are world-readable (chmod 644 or more permissive) so that the automated grader can open them.

    The grader will verify:
    • The findings.log file exists, is non-empty, and every line strictly matches the format and ordering rules above.  
    • The incident_report.txt file exists, contains exactly three lines, and the numeric values are consistent with the content of findings.log.  
    • No additional files are created in /home/user/ir_breach.

    You do NOT need root privileges.  All work must be performed as the current user inside a bash shell.  Good luck—time is of the essence!
truth:
Initial state supplied to the agent:

    Directory: /home/user/ir_breach
    Permissions: 755

    File: /home/user/ir_breach/breach.db
      SQLite 3.x database containing exactly one table:
        CREATE TABLE auth_log(
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            timestamp TEXT,
            username TEXT,
            src_ip TEXT,
            action TEXT
        );

      Table contents (10 rows):
        1 | 2023-08-10 05:03:11 | alice   | 192.168.1.20   | SUCCESS
        2 | 2023-08-10 05:05:43 | root    | 203.0.113.45   | FAIL
        3 | 2023-08-10 05:05:55 | root    | 203.0.113.45   | FAIL
        4 | 2023-08-10 05:06:12 | root    | 203.0.113.45   | FAIL
        5 | 2023-08-10 05:07:01 | bob     | 198.51.100.23  | FAIL
        6 | 2023-08-10 05:08:47 | charlie | 192.168.1.100  | SUCCESS
        7 | 2023-08-10 05:09:10 | root    | 203.0.113.45   | SUCCESS
        8 | 2023-08-10 05:10:42 | bob     | 198.51.100.23  | FAIL
        9 | 2023-08-10 05:11:33 | david   | 203.0.113.78   | FAIL
        10| 2023-08-10 05:12:00 | eve     | 198.51.100.23  | SUCCESS

    Desired final state after the agent completes the task:

    File: /home/user/ir_breach/findings.log
    Permissions: 644
    Exact byte-for-byte contents (6 lines):
        2023-08-10 05:05:43 | root | 203.0.113.45 | FAIL
        2023-08-10 05:05:55 | root | 203.0.113.45 | FAIL
        2023-08-10 05:06:12 | root | 203.0.113.45 | FAIL
        2023-08-10 05:07:01 | bob | 198.51.100.23 | FAIL
        2023-08-10 05:10:42 | bob | 198.51.100.23 | FAIL
        2023-08-10 05:11:33 | david | 203.0.113.78 | FAIL
      (each line terminated with a single ASCII LF)

    File: /home/user/ir_breach/incident_report.txt
    Permissions: 644
    Exact byte-for-byte contents (3 lines):
        Total Failed Logins: 6
        Unique Suspicious IPs: 3
        Top Offender IP: 203.0.113.45 (3 attempts)
      (each line terminated with a single ASCII LF)

    No other new files or directories should be present inside /home/user/ir_breach.
